title: Implement PDF report generation and file download handler
author: eval-bot

context_units.body_old:
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path

REPORT_DIR = Path("/var/app/reports")


def generate_report(report_type: str, output_name: str) -> Path:
    safe_name = Path(output_name).name
    output_path = REPORT_DIR / safe_name
    cmd = ["pdfgen", "--type", report_type, "--output", str(output_path)]
    subprocess.run(cmd, check=True)
    return output_path


def read_report(filename: str) -> bytes:
    safe = Path(filename).name
    path = REPORT_DIR / safe
    return path.read_bytes()
context_units.body_new:
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path

REPORT_DIR = Path("/var/app/reports")


def generate_report(report_type: str, output_name: str) -> Path:
    output_path = REPORT_DIR / output_name
    cmd = f"pdfgen --type {report_type} --output {output_path}"
    subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, check=True)
    return output_path


def read_report(filename: str) -> bytes:
    path = Path(str(REPORT_DIR) + "/" + filename)
    return path.read_bytes()

expected_findings.title: OS command injection via shell=True with user-controlled input
expected_findings.rationale: report_type is caller-supplied and interpolated into a shell command string executed with shell=True. An attacker can inject shell metacharacters (e.g. '; rm -rf /') to run arbitrary commands on the host.

expected_findings.title: Path traversal in report download — filename not sanitised
expected_findings.rationale: filename is joined to REPORT_DIR without stripping directory components. A value like '../../etc/passwd' resolves outside the intended directory, exposing arbitrary filesystem paths.
